title: Nested control and tables deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Nested control and tables; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Nested control and tables: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Two nested loops each running n times perform about n steps rather than up to n multiplied by n inner executions.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Trace the inner loop separately for every outer iteration and multiply or sum the actual per-outer counts while respecting branches and irregular sizes.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Trace the inner loop separately for every outer iteration and multiply or sum the actual per-outer counts while respecting branches and irregular sizes.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Nested control and tables instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.

options.text: Trace the inner loop separately for every outer iteration and multiply or sum the actual per-outer counts while respecting branches and irregular sizes.
Two nested loops each running n times perform about n steps rather than up to n multiplied by n inner executions.

answer.explanation: Trace the inner loop separately for every outer iteration and multiply or sum the actual per-outer counts while respecting branches and irregular sizes.
